1403.name: Workshop Basement
1403.description: You stand within a small, square basement below the workshop of Astorath. Bits of gems and stone are strewn about, freely mixed with shards of oddly shaped steel and lumps of solid iron. A strange, shifting opening upon a nearby wall beckons you inward.

485.name: Bottom of the Slope
485.description: Stones worn smooth by running water have been transported here to form a winding path through the forest, near the bottom of an overgrown slope. Up above the outlines of several stone structures can be seen.

3621.name: Guild Courtyard
3621.description: A single mammoth beige stone makes up the floor of this courtyard, broken only by the trunks of thickly-leaved wildflower trees growing straight through the rock. The entire area is bathed in a deep purple glow cast by a canopy of eldritch energy above. Convex walkways lead into three similar buildings set into the mountain to your north, northwest, and northeast.

3622.name: Warriors Guild
3622.description: A series of petrified wooden benches line the walls within the entrance to Vasdemeir's place of training. Hoisted upon the rear wall is a suit of thick silver armor, crossed by two huge ebony-bladed swords. The sounds of battle echo from the west.

npcs.name: Uru
npcs.description: a bit of a natural.  Only real warriors get past, one way or another.

npcs.name: Vasdemier, Warmaster
npcs.description: Vasdemier is a man of few words, and when he does speak, you listen.  It might be the deep baritone of his voice, or it might be the fact that disrespect will earn you a sound thrashing, but he is not a man to be

3624.name: Clerics Guild
3624.description: Stepping into the cleric's guild a tranquil vibration of sanctification seeps into your bones. Magnificently weaved tapestries bear the images of the temples throughout the land, adding a hint of wealth to the austere surroundings within this guild.

npcs.name: Fordte
npcs.description: stairs.  He keeps stoic watch over the small chapel, bringing the wrath of god down upon those who seek to defile its sanctity.

3626.name: Mages Guild
3626.description: You step into a large, open room. A cabinet of ivory and glass is mounted here, supported by thin wooden branches erupting from the pale blue stone wall. Into the buffed opaline floor is embedded a small white stone bearing an inscription. A door to the east leads to the study of Palytdai.

npcs.name: Aelriche
npcs.description: Clad in the flowing robes of her order, this striking female is more

3627.name: House of the Spirit
3627.description: A dark, windowless room lit only by the glowing form of Palytdai meditating upon a small, daemon-skin pillow in the center, the sturdy walls and ceiling bearing the deposits from eons of burning incense. Several bundles of old, cracked and torn scrolls line a set of bronze and marble shelves toward the back. The shadowy forms of several students can be seen kneeling before the master mage, deep in concentration.

368.name: HalfMoon Road
368.description: Passing beneath a bough of thickly leaved trees leaning o'er from above, the roughly cut road approaches a wide tunnel entrance as it thins to no more than a footpath.

369.name: HalfMoon Road
369.description: Halfmoon road continues in a steady descent to the north, and climbs slowly around the mountainside to the south. At thie vantage point a partial view of the lands laying westward can be seen, covered by rolling, darkened hills.

370.name: HalfMoon Road
370.description: The road becomes narrower here as it continues a slow curve around the nearby mountainside. It slowly descends, toward a wide tunnel opening ahead to the northwest, and ascends back up toward Napenthe.

371.name: HalfMoon Road
371.description: The road ambles along upon the overgrown mount, here underpassing a looming elder redwood before continuing northward. A look southward gains the view of steadily declining terrain, and a range of dark, forbidding hills far off in the distance.

372.name: HalfMoon Road
372.description: The rutted, overgrown path referred to as HalfMoon road passes a meager trail leading southward into Napenthe before continuing east and west along the mountainside. Overhead a pale, violet glow illuminates the underside of the local vegetation and wood.

373.name: HalfMoon Road
373.description: Tangled, dry branches swoop down from the surrounding terrain to darken the winding mountainside road as it makes its way behind Napenthe. To the west it meets a southern trail of cracked stonework before continuing, and eastward along the road gather fallen stones near a bridge crossing overhead.

374.name: HalfMoon Road
374.description: Turning northeast, the road here is dotted by fallen stones. A sturdy old bridge of wood and stone crosses overhead to the east, and HalfMoon road continues westward.

375.name: Lusian's End
375.description: Lusian ends here at the edge of HalfMoon road, which curves around the rising mountainside. A narrow trail veers westward, and Lusian ventures southward beneath a row of overhanging trees.

npcs.name: Drunken ogre
npcs.description: Ugh.  Big, dumb, ugly and DRUNK.  Several scars serve well to ruin the otherwise naturally wretched appearance this Ogre was obviously blessed with

376.name: Lusian Trail
376.description: The path of broken stones known as Lusian Trail dips sharply here, beneath several oddly shaped trees leaning out from the surrounding terrain to form a natural enclosure. The trail leads south, and north toward a wide, curving road.

377.name: Lusian Trail
377.description: You travel along a narrow, darkened section of Lusian Trail, midway along the semi-level shelf of land supporting the old settlement called Napenthe. To your east the terrain climbs, and a large Inn can be seen, while westward, the tops of several battered stone buildings are clearly visible as the shelf curves around the mountain. The trail here leads north and south.

npcs.name: Mercenary guard
npcs.description: Hired by the noble to guard against the chance of double-crossing from

npcs.name: Trader
npcs.description: He appears weary, yet fit, and has the weather-roughened features of a one who resides among the high peaks of the northern mountains.

378.name: Lusian Trail
378.description: The stonework beneath your feet along Lusian trail breaks here, into a collection of fragments slowly being overgrown by moss and weeds. A cleanly cut path rises slightly to the east, a small sign marking it as Alumbir Trail.

npcs.name: Olivad the beggar
npcs.description: but a far off stare as he passes by, hand extended...

379.name: Lusian Trail
379.description: A single, tremendous tree to the east overhangs the Lusian trail as it winds through this shelf of land upon the mountain. The head of a Falzior daemon is mounted upon a pole here, where a thin path descends westward toward a row of run-down stone buildings. Beneath your feet is a collection of interlocking stonework, cracked and well-traveled.

npcs.name: Loon
npcs.description: gich interbreeding, the Loon hops and scampers about on springy legs with long-taloned feet, its arms joined by drooping folds of skin which

npcs.name: Winged goblin
npcs.description: concentric circles.  Two oversized black wings sprout from its back, upon which it can drift once propelled by its short, muscular legs.

380.name: Falzior Road
380.description: Falzior road ventures west and east, heading downward through the older sections of Napenthe, here passing by a loud, reeking tavern. Broken bottles and pools of congealed blood decorate what a sign declares to be the Pale Ale Tavern's main entrance.

npcs.name: Wasted thief
npcs.description: A more sodden wench you've never seen.  The polluted reek of alcohol flows joyously from her dishevled clothing, and her feet cross one another frequently as she stumbles along, singing, taunting, teasing and

381.name: Falzior Road
381.description: The narrow, littered road called Falzior travels east and west through this section of Napenthe, passing a large tavern to the north and a small stone shop to the south. Eastward Falzior can be seen to turn northward toward the mountainside.

382.name: Falzior Road
382.description: Turning here underneath a natural canopy formed by the tangled branches of two elder trees, the Falzior road offers passage north, and both routes appear to pass entrances to the local tavern. Nestled within the trees to the west is a strange building of stone and steel. Something within an abandoned shop to the west catches your eye.

383.name: North Falzior Road
383.description: North Falzior ambles away from the steeply dropping slope to the south, passing the west-end of the local tavern, heading toward a nondescript building surrounded by several skulking figures. A fork turning west lays just ahead to the north.

npcs.name: Hooded mage
npcs.description: You see a thin, hooded figure carefully making his way by.

384.name: North Falzior Road
384.description: Falzior ends here, before an unkempt little building surrounded by beggars and thieves. Something barely qualifying as a path ventures to to the west through the foliage upon the mount, and the road offers passage southward, past the tavern's side entrance.

npcs.name: Forsaken
npcs.description: form and begins drifting silently, awaiting your next move.

385.name: Masindaar Path
385.description: A small, cloistered path breaks off from Falzior road to head westward through the burgeoning mountainside greenery. You can see the edge of a shrine just west, and the tavern across Falzior to the east.

386.name: Masindaar Path
386.description: The cluttered path known by the name Masindaar ends here, before a meager, downtrodden shrine surrounded by brush and trees. The path meanders eastwards, toward Falzior Road. To the north, the the edge of a small pit can be seen.

387.name: Indequat's Amiable Alignments
387.description: You step into the ramshackle shrine, peering about warily at the odd assortment of strangely glowing items laying in corners upon the dirty stone floor, which is covered in crudely drawn holy symbols. A sign hangs upon one wall.

389.name: Stone Shop
389.description: You stand within a simple, small shop. Hung along the walls are a series of aging tapestries which were, most likely, once fine pieces of art, but are now no more than tattered covers for the cracks in the walls. A counter runs the length of one wall, and behind it sit several hefty iron chests. A scale rests upon the counter, next to a beaten old abacus.

npcs.name: Rholeo
npcs.description: coins at a large counter.

npcs.name: Rholeo's assistant
npcs.description: best.  He even lets Rholeo count his paycheck for him.  Poor sap.

390.name: Tavern Hallway
390.description: A dim, shabby hallway leads you further into the tavern, and turns to the east. Innumerable brawls for forgotten reasons have passed through this hall by the looks of it. A side exit leads out onto Falzior Road.

391.name: Back Room
391.description: The reek here is unbelievable. Detrius imprisons the floor, and scrawled rambling has come to cover the walls. Warriors have stopped here to relieve themselves in a variety of manners for hundreds of years, no doubt. A small, dim tunnel opening is to the east.

npcs.name: Unbelievable reek
npcs.description: You don't so much see it as smell it - an odor so foul it's taken on a

392.name: Pale Ale Tavern
392.description: Setting foot into the tavern you are hit by the eyes of several patrons, as well as the dank reek of sweat, blood, bile and beer. Abstract paintings adorn the interior, depicting a battle-weary party on some form of journey toward immortality. A hallway north goes deeper into the tavern.

npcs.name: Nilvec the GrueSlayer
npcs.description: fights with grues - just for fun, not profit.  However as of late he's taken to the drink, and spends his time swilling ale and remembering the

npcs.name: Unbelievable reek
npcs.description: You don't so much see it as smell it - an odor so foul it's taken on a

393.name: Top of the Slope
393.description: The slope gradually levels here on a shelf of land midway up the mountain. A natural spring fills a shallow pool here among a gathering of fallen stones. Leaning at an angle is an old, weathered sign aside a wide trail leading north, toward several stone buildings. A smaller trail ventures east.

395.name: In the Trees
395.description: Thick, thorny vines dangle about as you maneuver through the branches of these elder trees. Up above can be seen the nests of the forest flocks. A variety of wildlife slithers and crawls about, some stopping to consider you and their hunger.

396.name: In the Trees
396.description: The tree branches surround you here, almost forming a natural tunnel. From above can be heard low voices and an occasional burst of laughter. Upon one particularly large, gnarled branch before you are some rough carvings.

399.name: Alumbir Trail
399.description: Alumbir trail curves eastward, just above an even smaller trail along the edge of the basin below. At this elevation the ancient basin trees yet obscure one's view, still looming far overhead.

400.name: Alumbir Trail
400.description: Here Alumbir splits, one fork climbing upward through the lush mountain vegetation, the other continuing eastward. A tiny creature made entirely of stone scampers by, peering up at you for a moment before diving into the greenery. Smoke drifts from the northeast. Alumbir Trail Alumbir trail curves eastward, just above an even smaller trail along the edge of the basin below. At this elevation the ancient basin trees yet obscure one's view, still looming far overhead.

npcs.name: Olivad the beggar
npcs.description: but a far off stare as he passes by, hand extended...

401.name: North Alumbir Trail
401.description: North Alumbir climbs the slowly rising terrain upon the mount, splitting here into two paths, one leading further upward toward a well-kept Inn, the other turning eastward along a narrow, overgrown path.

402.name: Alumbir Trail
402.description: The trail narrows here as it passes a tall stone shop, well kept yet obviously aged. The ground is quite well worn here. Eastward Alumbir turns past a mammoth stump. Above the flank of a large Inn can be made out, through a snarl of dense brush. Alumbir Trail Here Alumbir splits, one fork climbing upward through the lush mountain vegetation, the other continuing eastward. A tiny creature made entirely of stone scampers by, peering up at you for a moment before diving into the greenery. Smoke drifts from the northeast.

403.name: Stone Shop
403.description: An impressive array of gear fills Grelore's implements of exploration. Hung from the ceiling are a set of seven stained-glass mobiles back-lit by a lively fireplace to the rear of the shop. Several large, daemon-hide chairs offer patrons a sturdy seat while they wait for their orders to be filled.

404.name: Alumbir Trail
404.description: Here Alumbir passes by a strange building of misshappen stone just to the south, before continuing west and east. Although of stone, the structure appears soft, and an odd violet glow rolls over the surfaces from time to time. You notice the local residents maintaining a wide berth, and in fact the trail itself seems to afford this shop some distance.

405.name: Guild of the Bard
405.description: Instruments of song and war adorn the rough stone walls of an expansive guildhall, alongside worn tapestries depicting battles great and old. A large wooden door gives exit north to the streets of Napenthe. A kettle hangs within a fireplace, boiling stew and warming the guild. Bottles of ale litter the floor about a large round table toward the back of the hall.

npcs.name: Arixle
npcs.description: and the look on his face shows that he knows it well.

406.name: Alumbir Trail
406.description: Turning northeast, Alumbir passes by a huge, hollow treestump. Someone has left a small sign nearby. The terrain quickly drops to the south, and looking there you see the tangled mass of the basin woods. Alumbir ventures west toward a few small shops, eventually meeting Lusian Trail. You can't go that way.

407.name: Northeast Alumbir
407.description: The curve of Alumbir passes by a thicket of tall weeds here, heading northward toward the rising mountain, and turning southwest by a hollow treestump. You can see an old wooden bridge in the distance northwest.

408.name: Northeast Alumbir
408.description: The northeast portion of Alumbir pushes through thickening mountain growth, splitting here to travel north and east along a narrow, dusty trail. A handful of strangely marked buildings line the way, here by sign declared as Dhrajet Road.

npcs.name: Loon
npcs.description: gich interbreeding, the Loon hops and scampers about on springy legs with long-taloned feet, its arms joined by drooping folds of skin which

409.name: Along Dhrajet
409.description: A thin, dry trail beneath the trees forms what passes for Dhrajet street, just east of Alumbir Trail. Standing to the north is a small, odd structure of solid onyx. Dhrajet continues eastward toward a formidable stone building bearing several strange markings.

npcs.name: Ashane
npcs.description: He appears confused, bruised, and pauses occasionally to look up at the

npcs.name: Loon
npcs.description: gich interbreeding, the Loon hops and scampers about on springy legs with long-taloned feet, its arms joined by drooping folds of skin which

410.name: Dhrajet's End
410.description: The dusty street beneath the trees ends here before a modest structure of solid greystone. A small sign hangs above the rounded oak door, and the scent of Neroli drifts from within. Dhrajet ventures westward toward the more well- known areas of Napenthe.

411.name: Entrance to Astorath's Workshop
411.description: A well weaved and well traveled rug absorbs your steps as you enter the foyer of Astorath's workshop. Neroli burns upon a silver and black column placed in a corner, the drifting smoke made visible by a large, pale green light-globe stationed above toward the center of the room. Grey stone walls bear the embellishment of intertwined silver curves at their edges. Rounded arches lead north and south, while steps offer exits upward and downward.

412.name: Workshop
412.description: Immaculately crafted weaponry hangs upon the walls in this room. At a large black slab of stone a hooded mage chants and gesticulates, slowly bringing a loose pile of gems and stones into the shape of a mace. A figure steps out of the air before you, and asks if it may be of assistance. Upon the wall here is a sign.

npcs.name: Sheckie
npcs.description: Tough enough to avoid being skinned and used for armour by the workshop armourer, you wouldn't want to piss it off.

413.name: Workshop
413.description: Suits of exotic armor rest upon rigid skeletons posed in various acts of battle here. You could spend hours marveling over the detailed armor they wear, if it were not for their unsettling habit of coming to life to change position every few moments. A sign rests upon the wall.

npcs.name: Daemoncorpse
npcs.description: armourer, you wouldn't want to piss it off.

415.name: Northeast Alumbir
415.description: The northeastern Alumbir trail passes by a low, long building to the west here as it travels north and south through the richly overgrown mountainside. A carved wooden sign is hung upon the square stone door of the building.

416.name: Lowstone Hall
416.description: You stand within a narrow hall, the ceiling close overhead. A pair of worn, overstuffed chairs rest against the western wall, below a wide, fading painting. Silvered sconces line the hall, providing illumination. A solid door of redwood stands to the north. The main entrance lay just east.

417.name: Lowstone Hall
417.description: Past the heavy redwood door you enter a wide oval chamber, sparsely furnished and dimly lit. A few small chairs rest before a large stone table, where several odd objects lay. Behind the desk stands a cabinet fairly packed with old scrolls, books, and broken pieces of things you can't seem to identify.

npcs.name: Katyaviana
npcs.description: exists but cannot find.  The old crone seems somewhat startled by your sudden arrival, as though she had been expecting someone else.

418.name: SmallStone Path
418.description: Just outside a lowstone building set into the heart of Napenthe, this cobbled path of stones twists narrowly 'neath the boughs of mountainside trees around a shallow pond. Towards the end of the path an edge of old wrought iron can be seen hanging loosely by a pair of decaying stone pillars.

419.name: SmallStone Path
419.description: The smallstone path curves round a shallow pond, deeper into the overgrown mountainside of Napenthe. Twisted roots break through the loose stones collecting at water's edge in a soggy attempt to snag your steps. A sudden, forceful chill drifts from the northwest.

npcs.name: Katyaviana
npcs.description: exists but cannot find.  The old crone seems somewhat startled by your sudden arrival, as though she had been expecting someone else.

420.name: SmallStone Path
420.description: No more than a thin trickle of water flows down the rocky exposed surface of the mountainside as this path of small stones edges by, feeding the nearby pool. Directly to the west rest a pair of twisted iron gates, lazilly clutching onto the remains of two stone pillars, covered in a thousand year's mossy overgrowth.

421.name: Before the Graveyard Gates
421.description: A fertile mixture of spongy moss, twisted root and loose stone collects and ends here before two crumbling old pillars. A pair of ruined iron gates hang loosely between, albiet rusted quite tightly shut.

422.name: Northeast Alumbir
422.description: Nearing the HalfMoon bridge, Alumbir turns, offering passage to the east and to the south, where rests a small, curious stone house. Several of the nearby trees bear carved markings. A hint of smoke drifts by from the southeast, where can be seen the top of a strange onyx structure.

npcs.name: Olivad the beggar
npcs.description: but a far off stare as he passes by, hand extended...

423.name: Northeast Alumbir
423.description: Younger trees crowd the Alumbir trail here as it turns to travel to the west, and to the north toward HalfMoon road. A slight mist drifts from between the trees, which bear many carved markings.

424.name: Alumbirs End
424.description: You come to Alumbir's End, where HalfMoon road passes by the simple shop of Magellan the Mystic. Alumbir ventures southward into Napenthe, and the road can be seen to travel west and east. Above a wooden bridge crosses, leading upward onto the higher mountainside.

425.name: Magellan's Mystics
425.description: Through the open archway you enter a small, cluttered shop of magic. All about lay oddities from across the realms, some recognizable, others not. At one end of the shop rests a simple, wooden table covered in a velvet cloth, where Magellan works upon items brought to him.

npcs.name: Hooded mage
npcs.description: You see a thin, hooded figure carefully making his way by.

426.name: HalfMoon Road
426.description: A narrow trail meets HalfMoon road as it travels east and west along the mountainside, nearing an old wooden bridge connecting the settlement to the southwest to the slope above.

427.name: HalfMoon Road
427.description: Thickly leaved mountain trees line the curving HalfMoon road as it ventures northeastward near Napenthe. Scatterings of fallen leaves and dried nuts litter the roughcut road, collecting along the sides.

428.name: East Alumbir Trail
428.description: An overgrown, footworn trail passes below the Basilisk Inn above, near North Alumbir. To the east the trail ends at the edge of a small stone and wood bridge.

429.name: East Alumbir Trail
429.description: East Alumbir turns northeast through the brush and halts before a thin wooden bridge crossing HalfMoon road below. Through the dense growth here you glimpse the Basilisk Inn above, and several small structures below, to the south and southeast.

430.name: HalfMoon Bridge
430.description: The old bridge is small, but sturdy, and passes over a wide road skirting the rising mountainside. Still rise the elder forest trees, preventing what otherwise might be a spectacular view of the basin as it stretches out toward the southern lands, and the coast far west. Among the interwoven branches of the trees to the southwest is nestled a small Inn.

431.name: On the Mount
431.description: Upon the fertile mountain slope, a narrow wooden bridge crosses a wooded road below. A grouping of path-stones venture westward through a series of amethyst arches overgrown with ivy, and a footpath runs eastward toward a circular stand of trees.

461.name: Abandoned Shop
461.description: As you duck inside to escape yet another cutpurse, broken chairs, crusty cloth, and other trash crunch underfoot. Your eye first catches glints of green: vines poke through the detritus, their tendrils straining for the sparse light coming through a window of fractured glass. An especially nasty putrescence assaults your nostrils from a passageway crudely hacked into the southern wall. To the rear of the structure, steps tumble into darkness.

npcs.name: Zool
npcs.description: and of frame, but his burning eyes convey confidence in his ability to

462.name: Pit of Darkness
462.description: You find yourself at the bottom of a shallow pit within a bare stone chamber. Dark maroon stains cover the floor, and a few rotting piles of bones lie strewn about the pit. This is not a pleasant place, but it's ideal for training in the dark arts.

npcs.name: Forsaken
npcs.description: form and begins drifting silently, awaiting your next move.

463.name: Pale Ale Tavern
463.description: Several small, rounded tables are haphazardly arranged within the main room of the tavern, several feet away from the active bar. Bits of debris litter the floor, and various streaks and stains marr the paintings along the cracked stone walls.

3625.name: House of the Holy
3625.description: A low ceiling coupled with the thick output from several burning sticks of frangipani serve to smother you in this clerical study. Two diamonds of ornate stained glass glow with the light from the canopy outside, providing the room's only illumination.

397.name: Under the Canopy
397.description: A dense collection of intertwining branches give this place the feeling of indoors, rather than out. To the east, a small rounded opening in the branches beckons.

npcs.name: Malitoth
npcs.description: teeth. She hasn't a worry in the world.

398.name: Thieves' Bivouac
398.description: Nestled in the canopy here is a large, round area surrounded by mingled branches that form makeshift walls. A handful of shifty looking individuals lounge about, practicing their skills and partaking of a peculiar burning herb.

npcs.name: Stoned thief
npcs.description: with complete disinterest.  He giggles to himself, then breaks into full-blown laughter at a joke to which only he is privvy.

414.name: Workshop
414.description: Robes of sheer, transparent material are draped about on a multitude of smallish gargoyles around the room. Upon a hovering quartz shelf lies a collection of miscellaneous clothing, some of it glowing slightly. From above drops a wispy shade to ask if you'd care to try something on.

450.name: Library
450.description: You wander along a series of curving steps which seem to never end, passing row upon row of shattered and looted glass cabinets. Tremendous candles flank the steps, still burning, never melting. All save for a few of the cabinets seem to have been smashed and emptied.

457.name: Gallery
457.description: Steps end where the expanse of a spacious gallery begins. Paintings adorn the walls in elegantly molded silver frames. A number of thin, low pillars are tastefully arranged about the gallery, supporting crystal vases filled by dried wildflowers.

488.name: Inside a Fallen Tree
488.description: The inside of this tree is laced with the pungent odor of natural decay and the defecations of various creatures which have no doubt made use of this excellent hiding place, as the opening is too small for larger predators to enter. Insects have chewed away a substantial portion of the trunk, revealing a passage downward into the earth.

504.name: Path-stones
504.description: You step through a natural tunnel created by a mass of vines hanging down from the old growth here within the misty basin. Water is everywhere, drenching your clothing and your pack, and life is abundant despite the darkness surrounding you. Path-stones amble along the forest floor, heading north and west here.
